behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real speech-to-text model output (LLM-DEP). A stubbed transcript with perfect speaker tags would hide diarization/label-loss errors (MOCK-HIDE). No multi-agent sequence is required for this specific check, so Workflow is rejected.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real recorded multi-speaker meeting clip through the production speech-to-text API and assert the returned transcript preserves speaker segmentation/labels needed for downstream attendee attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic on transcript text to structured fields (MOCK-MISS says single-function/algorithm boundary). No required OS/network/subprocess and no dependence on real model judgment. Higher levels were considered and rejected because behavior text does not require real API/model interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript text fixtures containing explicit assignments, assert parser output includes correct assignee and task text fields in the summary input object.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real summarization model behavior mapping assignments to action items (LLM-DEP). Deterministic mocks can always return compliant output and hide omissions (MOCK-HIDE). Workflow was considered but rejected because this behavior can be validated with a single real summarization step from transcript input.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a real or realistic transcript with known per-attendee assignments to the live summarization API and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text targets routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified; this is deterministic schema placement logic (MOCK-MISS unit floor). No real infra or model output quality is required. Integration/Agentic rejected because behavior text disqualifies dependence on identification quality itself.
behaviors.test_description: Call the decision-routing function with pre-identified decision objects and assert they are placed under `decisions` schema fields and excluded from narrative buckets.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality behavior of the real summarization model: whether it surfaces decisions in the right section (LLM-DEP). Stubbed outputs hide real extraction/placement misses. Workflow considered but rejected because one model decision stage can validate this behavior directly.
behaviors.test_description: Submit transcripts with clearly stated decisions to the live summarization model and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` and not only in free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing across segments plus merged coherence, involving repeated model calls and handoff correctness across steps (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP). Lower levels miss cross-step degradation and merge failures. System alone is insufficient because the risk is not just infra but chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through production chunking/transcription/summarization flow; assert segment sequencing occurs, outputs are merged, and final summary remains globally coherent (no missing/duplicated segment-level decisions/actions).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with real audio upload, transcription model output, summarization model output, and final delivery. It crosses real infrastructure and multiple agentic steps; failures often emerge only in sequence/orchestration. Any lower level would miss true no-manual-intervention breakdowns.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run with a real meeting recording from upload through final structured delivery, asserting automatic completion, valid schema output, and no operator/manual retry steps.
